channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Philadelphians channel their inner Italian Stallion at Rocky and Adrian look-alike contest
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Couples who look awkward on skates — and on dates — had a chance to celebrate their cute clumsiness in a Rocky and Adrian look-alike contest at Philadelphia’s RockyFest on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to know about the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The masked gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson remained elusive Friday, even as investigators learned more about his movements before and after the shocking crime. Investigators still don't know his name. They haven't found the gun.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother of Austin Tice, journalist missing in Syria, says new information proves her son is alive
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The mother of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for more than a decade, said Friday that she was confident her son was alive, citing information she said had come from a “significant source” that she did not identify but said had been vetted by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man on trial in the killing of an Ole Miss student gave conflicting information, police say
item.description: The man on trial in the killing of University of Mississippi student Jimmie “Jay” Lee gave conflicting information to police about how well he and Lee knew each other, according to testimony Friday by an officer who helped lead the investigation.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about Northern California's rare tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Northern California was under a rare and brief tsunami warning alert Thursday that tested local emergency notification systems after a 7.0 magnitude earthquake shook part of the state.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge upholds use of race in Naval Academy admissions, saying a diverse military is stronger
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Friday ruled that the U.S. Naval Academy can continue considering race in its admissions process, finding that military cohesion and other national security factors mean the school should not be subjected to the same standards as civilian universities.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Newsom visits border, slams Trump on planned deportations, tariffs
item.description: "No state will be more impacted by that, no region will be impacted by that, small businesses that will lose customers, jobs that will be lost, inflationary impacts on every single one of you," said Newsom.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Crews recover the body of a woman from a Pennsylvania sinkhole after a 4-day search
item.description: Police say the remains of a woman who fell into a sinkhole have been recovered four days after she went missing while searching for her cat.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man in custody after locking himself inside Montgomery County church, deputies say
item.description: Deputies responded to a church in Montgomery County early Friday morning after a man walked into the building and locked himself inside.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, NewsNation

item.title: Biden lights National Christmas tree for last time as president
item.description: President Joe Biden presided over the lighting of the National Christmas tree for his final time as commander-in-chief on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's 'premeditated' attack and suspect's getaway: timeline
item.description: The NYPD is on the hunt for a masked assassin who killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson outside a Manhattan Hilton hotel Monday and escaped on a bike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tyre Sampson's family awarded $310 million after fatal fall on Florida ride
item.description: Tyre Sampson, 14, was tragically killed after falling to his death off a freefall tower drop ride at Orlando’s ICON Park.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting used fake ID at hostel: sources
item.description: The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in Midtown is believed to have used a fake New Jersey ID to check into an Upper West Side hostel before the shooting, sources told Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Company fined $172K after children employed to clean Iowa pork plant
item.description: They employed 11 children to "use corrosive cleaners to clean head splitters, jaw pullers, bandsaws, neck clippers and other equipment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, WGN 9

item.title: Killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO uncorks anger at insurance industry
item.description: The killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on a Manhattan street is the latest moment to shine a light on the tensions roiling American life.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: Who could see a white Christmas? Map shows which areas have best chance of snow
item.description: AccuWeather analyzed historical weather data between 1991 and 2020 to determine which areas of the country have the best odds of seeing a snowy Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing spotlights challenge of protecting leaders of companies
item.description: He’s one of the most famous and widely admired corporate leaders in the world. But it’s the haters that companies like Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta worry about.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fake bomb threat targets homes of insurance executive killed in NYC: Police
item.description: The threat was sent in an email to multiple people, who in turn alerted law enforcement, according to incident reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Things to know about the federal investigation into the Memphis Police Department
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A U.S. Department of Justice investigation found that the Memphis Police Department committed a host of civil rights violations, including using excessive force, making illegal traffic stops and disproportionately targeting Black people in the majority Black city.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Link to adult website on Mattel's 'Wicked' dolls at center of proposed class action lawsuit
item.description: A mother claims she and her daughter visited the link on the box, only to be "horrified" by what they saw, according to the lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: QVC recalls 1.1 million oven gloves after more than 90 burn injury reports
item.description: QVC is recalling more than 1 million pairs of oven gloves after dozens of people were burned while using them, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Secret Service leader, GOP rep get into shouting match over 9/11 questioning
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's killer used fake ID to check into NYC hostel before slaying: sources
item.description: The suspect wanted for the brutal slaying of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson used a fake ID to check into a New York City hostel before the brazen execution-style killing, police sources tell Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Children kidnapped from Augusta County found in Birmingham Alabama
item.description: UPDATE 10:44 A.M. 12/6 AUGUSTA COUNTY, Va. (WFXR) - The August County Sheriff's Office has released more details following the recovery of the three children abducted on December 5.
item.dc:creator: Thad Randazzo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shouting match erupts over 9/11 during Secret Service hearing
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Words on ammo in CEO shooting echo common phrase on insurer tactics: Delay, deny, defend
item.description: A message left at the scene of a health insurance executive’s fatal shooting — “deny,” “defend” and “depose” — echoes a phrase commonly used to describe insurer tactics to avoid paying claims.
item.dc:creator: TOM MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tsunami warning canceled after strong California earthquake
item.description: The National Weather Service canceled a tsunami warning Thursday for a large stretch of California coastline, saying the threat had passed following a strong earthquake near Eureka.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Splash pads associated with waterborne disease outbreaks: CDC
item.description: "Children are at increased risk for exposure to pathogens in contaminated splash pad water," the CDC said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Ammunition from UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting had words written on it: sources
item.description: Bullets found on the ground where UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson was shot Wednesday had writing on them, sources told Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Kala Rama, KRON4

item.title: Bitcoin hits $100,000 after Trump names pro-crypto SEC chair
item.description: The former president is bolstering the evolving platform amidst the threat of new currency systems seeking to challenge the U.S. dollar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects allegations
item.description: The offensive is among the deadliest and most destructive since World War II, and has destroyed vast areas of the besieged coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Police say searchers don’t expect to find woman in Pennsylvania sinkhole alive
item.description: UNITY TOWNSHIP, Pa. (AP) — The search for a woman who is believed to have fallen into a sinkhole in western Pennsylvania has become a recovery effort after two treacherous days of digging through mud and rock produced no signs of life, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: GENE PUSKAR and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New LAPD chief says he will work to protect immigrants ahead of Trump's plans for mass deportations
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The new chief of the Los Angeles police force said his department is working with consulate offices for Mexico and other Central American countries to draft a plan to protect immigrants ahead of the Trump administration’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio's new law aimed at 'hooning' takes effect
item.description: Ohio's new law, increases penalties for stunt driving, street racing, and evading police, and allows for the seizure of vehicles involved, in response to growing concerns over hazardous driving practices and loud traffic disturbances across the state.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Thompson, KRON4

item.title: Search suspended for man believed to have gone overboard from cruise ship off California's coast
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard said Tuesday that it has suspended the search for a man believed to have gone overboard from a cruise ship as it was returning to San Francisco after a voyage to Ensenada, Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
